verdict.summary: Small, correct maintenance PR. It hardens the MSRV gate by adding --all-targets to the MSRV `cargo check` so test targets must also compile at the declared floor, then fixes the two test-only sites that relied on temporary-lifetime-extension: `TransportAsReader(&Canned(body))` in sparq-engine-service and `WidgetContext { value: Some(&lit("a")), .. }` in sparq-forms. Both are fixed the same, robust way — bind the temporary to a named local (`let transport = ...` / `let value = ...`) so its lifetime is explicit and version-independent rather than relying on extension through a constructor. Tests are behavior-preserving (identical assertions, exercise the real path, not vacuous). No hard-coded perf numbers, no honesty/soundness surface, no ZK/MPC claims, no security concern. The only residual risk is that --all-targets at MSRV could surface further test-only breakages elsewhere in the workspace, but that is exactly what the gate is meant to catch and CI is authoritative here; the two fixes are self-contained and complete.
